target: generate-html.js — Evaluation Guide Principle 3 rewording + eclipse discrimination window acknowledgment
kernel_of_truth: The curmudgeon's ISS-821 critique identifies the single strongest methodological vulnerability in our review: we claim not to assume mainstream physics is correct, while using mainstream-derived measurements (NOAA magnetometry, Gaia astrometry, WGS84 coordinates) as our reference standard for every comparison. This IS an asymmetry. It's a defensible asymmetry — instrumental measurements are more robust than cosmological interpretation — but calling it 'not assuming mainstream physics is correct by default' oversells our neutrality. The dome defender who notices this has a legitimate process objection. For ISS-900: the curmudgeon is also right that the 17-21 nT (dome) vs 5-20 nT (Chapman) overlap creates a narrow 1 nT discrimination window. Acknowledging this is honest and actually strengthens our argument — it shows the dome's 'independent prediction' barely differs from standard physics because it was derived FROM standard physics data.

patches.description: Reword Principle 3 heading and body to accurately describe our methodological stance without overselling neutrality
patches.old_text: 3. Mainstream physics is not assumed correct by default. We do not dismiss the dome model simply because it disagrees with established science. Instead, we ask: does this specific prediction match this specific measurement? When we say "the globe model explains this," we mean the quantitative prediction from standard physics matches the observed data — not that the standard model must be right because it is standard.
patches.new_text: 3. Claims are evaluated against measurement, not against theoretical authority. We do not reject the dome's predictions simply because they contradict established science. Instead, we ask: does this specific prediction match this specific measurement? When we say "the globe model explains this," we mean the quantitative prediction from standard physics matches the observed data — not that the standard model must be right because it is standard. We acknowledge that our reference measurements (NOAA magnetometry, Gaia astrometry, IGRF models, GPS timing) were developed within a spherical-Earth framework. We use them because they are the most precisely calibrated instruments available, and both models claim to be consistent with their readings. This is not cosmological neutrality — it is empirical pragmatism: the raw instrumental outputs are physical facts that any viable model must account for.
patches.rationale: The old heading ('Mainstream physics is not assumed correct by default') claimed a neutrality the review cannot deliver — every comparison uses mainstream-derived measurements. The new heading ('evaluated against measurement, not theoretical authority') is more precise: we're measuring against instruments, not against cosmological consensus. The added sentence about measurement infrastructure explicitly acknowledges the asymmetry the curmudgeon identified, then defends it on correct grounds (instrumental calibration, not cosmological commitment). This also closes the defense path where a dome supporter says 'your review claims neutrality while relying entirely on globe-physics measurements.'

patches.description: Update TLDR to match new Principle 3 wording
patches.old_text: we don't assume mainstream physics is correct by default
patches.new_text: claims are evaluated against measurement not theoretical authority
patches.rationale: The TLDR must match the principle heading. The old phrasing ('don't assume mainstream physics is correct') is the exact overclaim the curmudgeon flagged.

patches.description: Acknowledge narrow discrimination window in eclipse pre-commitment
patches.old_text: If they fall within both ranges, the result supports standard physics, not dome geometry, because the dome's prediction values were derived by scaling Chapman-mechanism data. We state this now, before the eclipse, so there is no ambiguity afterward.
patches.new_text: If they fall within both ranges, the result supports standard physics, not dome geometry, because the dome's prediction values were derived by scaling Chapman-mechanism data. We note that the ranges overlap substantially: any measurement between 17 and 20 nT in magnitude falls within both the dome's range and the Chapman range, and only values above 20 nT would favor the dome. The discriminating window is approximately 1 nT — a reflection of how little the dome's prediction actually differs from the standard model it claims to replace. We state this now, before the eclipse, so there is no ambiguity afterward.
patches.rationale: The curmudgeon correctly identified that the discrimination window is only ~1 nT wide. Rather than hiding this, acknowledging it turns a potential process objection ('you defined Chapman's range to overlap') into a substantive argument: the dome's 'independent' prediction barely differs from standard physics because it was derived from Chapman data. Transparency here preempts the 'rigged test' attack.

holes_addressed: ISS-821: Principle 3 overclaims cosmological neutrality while using mainstream measurements
ISS-900: Eclipse discrimination window narrowness unacknowledged
new_evidence_added: Explicit acknowledgment of measurement infrastructure asymmetry (NOAA, Gaia, IGRF, GPS) in Principle 3
Quantification of eclipse discrimination window (~1 nT) in falsifiability section

anticipated_objections.objection: The new Principle 3 wording admits bias — a dome defender will quote 'This is not cosmological neutrality' as a concession
anticipated_objections.response: Let them. The full sentence says 'This is not cosmological neutrality — it is empirical pragmatism: the raw instrumental outputs are physical facts that any viable model must account for.' Admitting we use mainstream instruments while explaining WHY is stronger than false claims of neutrality. The dome model itself uses the same NOAA/IGRF data for its monitoring system. If the dome's own automated scripts trust mainstream instrumentation, the dome defender cannot credibly object to our reliance on it.

anticipated_objections.objection: Acknowledging the 1 nT eclipse window weakens our falsifiability claim
anticipated_objections.response: The opposite. A test with a 1 nT discrimination window is a FEATURE of our argument, not a bug. It demonstrates that the dome's 'independent' prediction barely differs from standard physics. If the dome model truly described a different physical reality (toroidal fields, aetheric medium), its eclipse predictions should diverge dramatically from Chapman — not overlap by 95%. The narrowness is evidence of derivative methodology, not independent physics.

anticipated_objections.objection: The curmudgeon also flagged Principles 1-2 as nested/redundant — why not fix that too?
anticipated_objections.response: The 1-2 overlap is a presentation weakness (minor severity), not a logical error. Both principles are correctly applied to every WIN regardless of how they're numbered. Rewording Principle 3 is higher priority because it's a genuine overclaim that a dome defender can exploit. The 1-2 overlap can be addressed in a future polish pass if desired.

note_to_decider: Both patches target generate-html.js (infrastructure file). PATCH-1 and PATCH-1b modify the Evaluation Guide principles section. PATCH-2 modifies the eclipse pre-commitment paragraph in eg-falsifiability. All three are surgical text replacements. After applying, rebuild with 'node build.js html' and verify the Evaluation Guide tab renders correctly. The TLDR update (PATCH-1b) uses a partial string match — search for the old phrasing within the longer TLDR string on line 1348.
